name: Distill documents with Airtable and Telegram hands-free

options.systemPromptTemplate: You are a precise extraction engine. Only extract values present in the text.

parameters.text: =✅ Distill documents with Airtable and Telegram hands-free — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### On Telegram Event

Native push trigger — telegram calls this workflow the moment the event happens (no polling).

Next: Collect Raw Text

🔑 Telegram bot token

### Collect Raw Text

Shapes the item: writes `content`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Extract Structured Fields

### Extract Structured Fields

Pulls structured fields (requestId, query, context) out of free text using a strict extraction prompt.

Ports: ①→ All Required Fields Found? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Extract Structured Fields** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### All Required Fields Found?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.output?.requestId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Upsert into Airtable · ②→ Queue for Manual Review

### Upsert into Airtable

Upserts into Airtable's 'Requests' table, matched on `requestId` — safe to re-run without creating duplicates.

Ports: ①→ Merge Review Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Queue for Manual Review

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

Ports: ①→ Merge Review Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Merge Review Paths

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 25 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Guard Non-Empty query

### Guard Non-Empty query

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Airtable)

### Append Audit Row (Airtable)

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Requests' table.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ai-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ai-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Distill documents with Airtable and Telegram hands-free

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Distill documents with Airtable and Telegram hands-free. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual multi-tool agent is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
On Telegram Event → Collect Raw Text → Extract Structured Fields → All Required Fields Found? → Upsert into Airtable → Merge Review Paths → Save Execution Metadata → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating multi-tool agent in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Standardizes the process every time
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; Groq API key; Slack OAuth2; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The required-fields check runs *before* the write, not after — an incomplete extraction goes to manual review instead of landing a half-populated record in the system of record. Upsert (not plain create) means re-processing the same document twice updates the existing record instead of duplicating it.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Add a new field to the extraction schema and mark it required — watch more documents route to manual review.
2. Feed it a document deliberately missing a required field and trace exactly where it ends up.
3. Change the extraction target (e.g. invoice → contract fields) and adjust the upsert key to match.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your multi-tool agent process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.

json.query: What's our refund policy for orders over $500?
